cases.title: Prenatal screens coded diagnostic instead of preventive (ACA $0)
cases.persona: Pregnant, commercial Anthem BCBS-TX, in-network hospital lab. Routine prenatal blood screens she thought were free under her plan came back as a $2,390 bill. Already filed one appeal, got denied. Confused and frustrated; not a billing person.

turns.text: I'm pregnant and went in for routine prenatal blood work at my hospital — the standard stuff they make everyone do early on. I was told preventive pregnancy care was supposed to be free. Now I owe $2,390 and when I appealed, they denied it and said the labs were billed as 'diagnostic.' I don't even know what that means. Did I do something wrong? Why am I being charged for screenings everyone gets?

doc.name: lab-eob.pdf
lines: ANTHEM BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD OF TEXAS
EXPLANATION OF BENEFITS - THIS IS NOT A BILL
Member: Maria T.    Group: Fully Insured PPO
Rh factor / antibody screen   billed type: DIAGNOSTIC
Hepatitis B surface antigen   billed type: DIAGNOSTIC
Hepatitis C antibody          billed type: DIAGNOSTIC
Appeal status: INTERNAL APPEAL DENIED - billed as diagnostic per plan

turns.text: So what do I actually do next? My appeal already got denied once.

cases.title: Re-billed $1,093 a year after settling and paying the EOB
cases.persona: Recent widow, commercial BCBS-IL, in-network hospital. Her late husband's hospital claim was settled and she paid her share over a year ago. Now a revised bill arrives demanding $1,093 more on a claim she thought was closed. Grieving, alarmed, has the old paperwork.

turns.text: My husband passed away over a year ago. His hospital bill was all sorted out back then — the insurance processed it and I paid my part, $823.15, and I have the receipt. Now, fourteen months later, I just got a NEW bill from the same hospital saying I owe another $1,093.16 on that same stay. How can they come back this long after and charge more? I already paid what they told me. Do I have to pay this?

doc.name: revised-bill.pdf
lines: Guarantor: Eleanor R.   Patient: (deceased) Frank R.
Original date of service: 03/2025   Statement date: 05/2026
Insurer: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Illinois (IN-NETWORK)
*** ACCOUNT ADJUSTED - additional balance now due ***

turns.text: So what do I actually do? I don't want this going to collections but it feels wrong.

cases.title: One colonoscopy billed as two via a modifier; $4,047 owed vs $2,381 estimate
cases.persona: Commercial Aetna, in-network at Northwestern. Got a pre-service estimate of $2,381 patient cost for one colonoscopy. The bill came back showing two near-identical $5,466 procedure lines and a $4,047 balance. Both appeals already denied. Angry, feels misled by the estimate.

turns.text: I had ONE colonoscopy. Before the procedure they gave me a written estimate saying my share would be about $2,381. The actual bill shows what looks like the same procedure listed twice and now they say I owe $4,047. I already appealed with both the hospital and Aetna and both said the bill is 'accurate.' How is one colonoscopy two procedures? And how did $2,381 turn into $4,047?
Both appeals already failed. So what do I actually do next that might work?

cases.title: Surgical-tray add-on charged on a $0 preventive colonoscopy
cases.persona: Commercial ACA-marketplace BCBS-IL, in-network GI group. Went in for a routine screening colonoscopy he expected to be free. Got a bill for a $250 'surgical tray' supply charge applied to his deductible. Suspects it shouldn't be there but isn't sure.

turns.text: I went in for a routine screening colonoscopy — the preventive kind that's supposed to be 100% free under my plan. The colonoscopy itself was covered at $0 like I expected. But there's this extra $250 'surgical tray' charge they applied to my deductible. It's a screening I was told costs me nothing. Can they tack on a supply fee like that? It feels like a loophole.

doc.name: gi-statement.pdf
lines: Patient: Marcus W.   Plan: BCBS-IL Marketplace (IN-NETWORK)
Procedure billed as PREVENTIVE / ROUTINE SCREENING

turns.text: So what do I actually do to get this taken off?

cases.title: Annual-physical labs coded diagnostic and routed to a hospital lab; $1,223
cases.persona: Commercial ACA-marketplace BCBS-IL, in-network clinic, but labs sent to a hospital lab. Went in for a routine annual physical expecting free preventive labs. Got a $1,223 lab bill at 50% coinsurance. Appealed once; denied because the visit included medication monitoring. Confused about where the line is.

turns.text: I went in for my yearly physical, the routine checkup that's supposed to be free preventive care. They drew blood like always. But the labs got sent to a hospital lab and now I owe $1,223 because my plan only covered them at 50%. I appealed and got denied — they said because my doctor also checks my blood pressure meds during the visit, the labs count as 'monitoring,' not preventive. I don't get it. It was my annual physical. Why am I paying $1,223?

doc.name: physical-eob.pdf
lines: BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD OF ILLINOIS
EXPLANATION OF BENEFITS - NOT A BILL
Member: Priya N.   Plan: Marketplace Silver
Visit: Annual physical (preventive)  Provider: UI Health Clinic (IN-NETWORK)
Labs processed at: UI HEALTH HOSPITAL LABORATORY
Lab panel - billed type: DIAGNOSTIC (medication monitoring)
Appeal status: DENIED - visit included medication monitoring, labs not purely preventive

turns.text: So what do I actually do now? The appeal already got turned down.
